batches.description: Statewide repositories, ethnic databases, research guides — v3.1 migration
batches.notes: URLs should be verified before production use
Bladen included from seed with full data. Colonial counties Beaufort (1705) and Bertie (1722) have records to 1696 and 1720 respectively.
Burned counties: Buncombe (1830/1865), Burke (1865 Stoneman's Raid — severe), Cabarrus (1874/1876 — limited), Camden (1868). Carteret has deeds from 1713.
Cherokee burned (3 courthouse fires: 1865, 1895, 1926). Clay burned (1870). Chowan (1668 precinct) has deeds from 1695.

Cumberland included from seed with full data. Craven (1712) burned in Tuscarora War, deeds from 1739. Currituck (1668 precinct) burned 1842, deeds recopied from 1696. Dare youngest eastern county (1870).
Duplin included from seed with full data. Davidson burned (1865 — partial). Edgecombe (1741) has deeds from 1732 (precinct era). Durham formed from Orange+Wake 1881.
Greene burned (1876). Granville (1746) has deeds from formation — colonial county. Forsyth: Moravian Archives (1753+) are exceptional. Franklin formed from extinct Bute County 1779.
Guilford minor fire (1872). Harnett burned (1892/1894). Hertford severely burned (1830+1862 — one of NC's most damaged). Halifax (1758) has deeds from 1729. Guilford Quaker records from 1754.

Hyde (1705) burned but records from 1713 survived. Iredell partial fire (1854). Johnston burned (records lost via Lenoir Co fires 1878/1880). Jones burned (1862 Civil War). Johnston Heritage Center is exceptional.
Lenoir burned (1878/1880 — deeds from 1737 survived). Madison burned (1865 Civil War). Martin burned (1862/1884). Lincoln from extinct Tryon. Macon formed 1828 from Haywood.
Montgomery triple-burned (1835/1840/1886). Mitchell partial fire (1907). Mecklenburg excellent online portal from 1763. Rowan colonial backcountry capital. Nash formed from Edgecombe 1777.
Onslow burned (1752/1755 hurricanes — storm damage). Orange important colonial county (1752) — Regulator Movement center. New Hanover colonial port deeds from 1729. Pamlico formed 1872.

Pasquotank (1668 precinct) deeds from 1700. Perquimans (1668 precinct) deeds from 1681 — among NC's earliest. Quaker records significant. Pitt formed from Beaufort 1760. Polk mountain county formed 1855.

Rowan (1753) — colonial backcountry capital with exceptional records. Randolph Quaker settlement. Robeson — Lumbee Tribe home county. Rutherford from extinct Tryon 1779. All unburned with good preservation.
Sampson included from seed with full data (burned — 1865/1921). Scotland youngest (1899). Stanly parent Montgomery triple-burned. Surry — Mount Airy/Mayberry. Stokes formed from Surry 1789.
Swain — Qualla Boundary/Cherokee tribal records. Tyrrell (1729) colonial deeds from 1735. Transylvania 'Land of Waterfalls'. Union fastest-growing. Vance formed 1881 from three counties.

Wake — state capital, NC State Archives location. Washington burned (1864 Battle of Plymouth). Warren plantation county. Watauga — Daniel Boone territory. Wilkes — Tom Dula murder. Yancey — Mount Mitchell.

migration_note: Migrated to v3.1 on 2026-03-10. Fields renamed: rod_* → online_land_records_*. Seed file removed (data duplicated in alphabetical batches). Statewide file created.
